text.title: file-reference-passing Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for passing refs instead of content between agent stages.

rule.statement: Any stage boundary carrying more than ~1K tokens of content MUST be refactored to pass references (paths, IDs, URLs) instead; content gets loaded lazily by the consumer.
rule.rationale: Content carried across stages consumes prompt budget twice. Subagents that return summaries with refs run at fixed parent cost regardless of corpus size.

rule.statement: Refs emitted by the model MUST be constrained to a known manifest (enum or regex over a provided list); never let the model invent ref strings.
rule.rationale: Without a manifest constraint, the model invents plausible-looking paths that do not exist; downstream load fails or, worse, loads the wrong file.

rule.statement: After each scan/select stage, every ref MUST be validated against the underlying store before passing to the next stage; on hallucinated ref, retry with the failure explicit, do NOT silently drop.
rule.rationale: Silent drops mask bugs; explicit retries with the bad-ref string in the prompt let the model self-correct.

rule.statement: If the underlying resource is mutable or expirable (remote URL, deletable doc, reassigned ID), the pipeline MUST snapshot the resource at scan time and reference the snapshot, not the live source.
rule.rationale: Reruns days later fail because the URL no longer resolves; snapshotting decouples the pipeline from external lifecycle.

rule.statement: Subagents MUST return a slim summary (3-5 sentences) plus a `refs: list[str]` of interesting pointers; they MUST NOT return raw file contents.
rule.rationale: Subagent context is isolated from the parent. Returning raw content forces the parent to summarise again, doubling cost; returning refs lets the parent re-read only what it needs.
